title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A person who works in a hospital and helps sick people is a _____.
explanation: A nurse works in a hospital taking care of patients.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The place where you go to borrow books is a _____.
explanation: A library is a public building where you can borrow and read books.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A formal written test that checks what students have learned is called an _____.
explanation: An exam is a formal test. The other options are not real English nouns.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: Your teacher gives you _____ to do at home after school.
explanation: Homework is an uncountable noun: no plural ('homeworks') and no 'a homework'. The standard spelling is one word: homework.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ fixes cars and other vehicles.
explanation: A mechanic is a person whose job is repairing engines and machines, especially in cars.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She got a very good _____ on her report card this semester.
explanation: 'A very good' requires a singular noun: 'grade'. The other options are wrong number or non-noun forms.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He likes to _____ photos of birds in the park.
explanation: The verb after 'to' must be the base form. 'Take photos' is the standard collocation in English.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: My _____ at work is to answer the phone and greet visitors.
explanation: In this sentence, 'job' means your duty or task — what you are responsible for doing.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ works in a kitchen and prepares food for customers.
explanation: A chef is a professional cook who works in a restaurant kitchen.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: In British schools, one of the three parts the school year is divided into is called a _____.
explanation: A term is one of the periods the school year is divided into (British English). The other options are not nouns for this meaning.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She enjoys _____ in her free time — she makes sweaters and scarves.
explanation: Knitting is the hobby of making clothes from wool using two long needles.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ delivers letters and packages to your home.
explanation: A postman (or mail carrier) is the person who brings mail to houses.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: I need to _____ for my exam tomorrow, so I can't go out tonight.
explanation: The base form after 'to' is 'study'. 'Studys' and 'studing' are misspellings; 'to study' would give 'need to to study'.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He works as a _____ — he builds houses and other structures.
explanation: A builder is a person whose job is constructing buildings and houses.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: Playing chess is a good way to improve your _____ skills.
explanation: Chess requires strategy and logic, so it improves your thinking skills.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ works in a school and teaches children.
explanation: A teacher is the person employed by a school to educate students in a classroom.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She has a _____ for languages — she already speaks three fluently.
explanation: A talent is a natural ability to do something well without much training.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He _____ his own business selling handmade furniture.
explanation: To 'run a business' means to manage and operate it. This is a common collocation.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The students are working on a group _____ about climate change.
explanation: A project is a piece of planned work or research done by students, often in a group.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: My favorite _____ at school is history.
explanation: A subject is an area of knowledge studied at school. The other options are not the correct noun form.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: Working the standard 40 hours a week, Monday to Friday, 9 to 5, is called working _____.
explanation: Full-time means working the standard number of weekly hours. The other options are not real English word forms.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ designs buildings and other structures.
explanation: An architect is a professional who plans and designs buildings before they are built.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He enjoys _____ — he goes to the lake every weekend with his fishing rod.
explanation: Fishing is the activity of catching fish, usually with a rod and line.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: I have a _____ with my boss at 2 o'clock to discuss next quarter's budget.
explanation: A meeting is a planned gathering at work where people discuss business topics. Lessons and lectures happen in classrooms, and recess is a school break.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She is very good at _____ — she can draw realistic portraits.
explanation: Art includes drawing, painting, and sculpture. Drawing realistic portraits is an art skill.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The _____ in our office is friendly and helpful — she answers all calls.
explanation: A receptionist is the person at the front desk who greets visitors and answers phones.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He _____ the guitar in a band on weekends.
explanation: We say 'play' with musical instruments: play the guitar, play the piano, play the drums.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: After university, she got a _____ as a software developer.
explanation: 'Job' is countable, so 'a job' is correct. 'Work' is uncountable and cannot follow 'a'; 'works' and 'worked' are not nouns that fit here.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: Students must wear a school _____ — a white shirt and dark trousers.
explanation: A uniform is the special set of clothes that students at some schools must wear.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He _____ a lot of time gardening — it's his favorite hobby.
explanation: The standard collocation is 'spend time'. With 'he', the correct third-person form is 'spends'; 'spended' is not a word and 'is spend' is ungrammatical.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ takes care of animals when they are sick.
explanation: A vet (veterinarian) is a doctor trained to treat animals, not people.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The company _____ 200 people in its main office.
explanation: Third-person singular 'the company' requires 'employs'. 'Employ' is wrong agreement, 'employing' is not a finite verb, and 'employed by' reverses the subject.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She is taking a photography _____ that lasts twelve weeks.
explanation: 'A' requires a singular noun: 'a course'. 'Courses' is plural, and 'coursing' and 'coursed' are not nouns that fit here.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: His _____ is to become a doctor one day.
explanation: 'His' plus singular 'is' requires the singular noun 'goal'. The other options are wrong number or non-nouns.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: We have a lunch _____ from 12:30 to 1:00 at work.
explanation: 'Lunch break' is the standard collocation. The other options are not nouns that fit this meaning.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She is very _____ with her hands — she can build and fix almost anything.
explanation: Skilled means having the ability and experience to do something well, especially practical tasks.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ works at a checkout in a supermarket.
explanation: A cashier is the person who handles payments at a register in a shop or supermarket.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He goes _____ every morning before work — he runs 5 kilometers.
explanation: Jogging is running at a slow, steady pace for exercise.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The teacher asked us to hand in our _____ by Friday.
explanation: An assignment is a piece of work given by a teacher with a deadline.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She works as a _____ — she writes articles for a newspaper.
explanation: A journalist reports news and writes articles for newspapers, magazines, or websites.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: I usually _____ work at half past eight in the morning.
explanation: The simple present with 'I' is 'start'. The other forms are wrong person or non-finite.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He is learning to play the _____ — he practices every evening.
explanation: The piano is a specific musical instrument. You learn to play a specific instrument, not 'instrument' in general.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: A _____ helps people with legal problems.
explanation: A lawyer is a professional who gives legal advice and represents people in court.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She _____ yoga every Tuesday and Thursday evening.
explanation: We say 'do yoga' (and 'do' with most individual exercises). We use 'play' for sports with a ball or competition.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The students had to give a _____ in front of the whole class, using slides and speaking for ten minutes.
explanation: 'Presentation' is the noun. 'Presentate' is not a word, 'presenting' is a gerund not a noun after 'a', and 'presentational' is an adjective.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He wants to _____ as a pilot when he grows up.
explanation: 'Work as' means to have a particular job or profession. 'Job' and 'career' are nouns, not verbs.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She collects _____ from every country she visits — she puts them in a special album.
explanation: Collecting stamps (postage stamps) from different countries is a classic hobby, and stamps are kept in albums.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: The _____ told us to open our textbooks to page 45.
explanation: In a classroom, the teacher gives instructions to students about their textbooks.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: He has good _____ skills — he works well with other people.
explanation: Teamwork skills mean you can cooperate and work effectively with others.

title: Work, Education & Hobbies
question: She got a _____ at work — now she is the department manager.
explanation: 'A' requires a noun: 'promotion'. 'Promote' is a verb, 'promoted' is a past participle, and 'promoting' is a gerund — none work after 'a' here.
